articles.title: Demonetized Cash Treated as Benami Property as Funds Were Routed Through Third-Party Account
articles.summary: The Appellate Tribunal under SAFEMA ruled that demonetized cash routed through another person’s bank account qualifies as benami property under the PBPT Act. The Tribunal held that cash can be treated as 'property' and attachment can continue based on the remaining benefit retained. This decision strengthens enforcement against money laundering and black money transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The Appellate Tribunal under SAFEMA held that routing demonetized cash through another person’s bank account constituted a benami transaction under the PBPT Act. The Tribunal ruled that cash qualifies as “property” and attachment could continue to the extent of the remaining benefit retained. The post Demonetized Cash Treated as Benami Property as Funds Were Routed Through Third-Party Account appeared first on TaxGuru .

The ruling has implications for how such transactions are taxed in India.

articles.title: Gujarat HC Quashed Section 148 Notice as Seized Material Had No Link With Assessee
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court set aside a Section 148 income tax reassessment notice after finding that seized documents from 2017 had no connection to a land transaction from 2021. The court ruled that without a clear link between the material and the assessee, the reassessment lacked validity. This decision impacts how tax authorities can use seized documents for reassessment purposes. The ruling underscores the need for a live nexus between seized material and the transaction under scrutiny.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that a document dated 2017 could not reasonably be linked to a land transaction executed in 2021 without supporting evidence. The absence of a live nexus rendered the reassessment invalid. The post Gujarat HC Quashed Section 148 Notice as Seized Material Had No Link With Assessee appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Pension Withheld Permanently Following Conviction – No Show-Cause Notice, No Opportunity of Being Heard; Rajasthan HC Quashes Unreasoned Order
articles.summary: The Rajasthan High Court set aside an order permanently withholding a government employee's pension following a conviction, citing the lack of a show-cause notice and opportunity of being heard. The court emphasized that pension is a statutory right and procedural safeguards like hearings are essential to prevent arbitrary deprivation. The ruling underscores the importance of due process in administrative actions affecting civil rights.
articles.raw_summary: Pension is not a bounty but a valuable statutory right earned by an employee after rendering long years of service and any order resulting in deprivation or permanent withholding of pension entails serious civil consequences and requirement of hearing is not an empty ritual but a substantive safeguard to ensure fair exercise of discretion by the authority. articles.title: Why the Sabarimala verdict allowing women into the temple differs from the hijab ‘ban’
articles.summary: The article compares the Supreme Court's verdict allowing women of all ages into the Sabarimala temple with the debate over the hijab ban in Karnataka. It argues that the Sabarimala ruling prioritizes individual autonomy, while the hijab ban is framed around biological differences. Both issues stem from patriarchal and religious influences. The verdict is a legal action with implications for gender rights and welfare policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: Both are practices shaped by patriarchy and religion. But one is determined by individual autonomy and the other by biology.

articles.title: ITC Cannot Be Blocked Through Mere Email Without Formal Order and Recorded Reasons: P&H HC
articles.summary: The Punjab & Haryana High Court ruled that the Goods and Services Tax (GST) authorities cannot block a taxpayer's Input Tax Credit (ITC) through a mere email without issuing a formal order and providing recorded reasons. The court directed the release of the blocked ITC, stating that such action violated Rule 86A of the CGST Rules. This ruling underscores the need for procedural fairness in tax administration and could impact how GST authorities enforce ITC restrictions in the future.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that mere email communication blocking ITC without a formal order and recorded reasons contravened Rule 86A of the CGST Rules. The blocked input tax credit was directed to be released. The post ITC Cannot Be Blocked Through Mere Email Without Formal Order and Recorded Reasons: P H HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: NCLAT Upholds Section 9 Admission as Separate Supply Obligations Created Operational Debt
articles.summary: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) ruled that unpaid supply dues between parties in a joint venture arrangement qualify as operational debt under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The tribunal held that separate supply agreements created distinct payment obligations, allowing insolvency proceedings despite the joint venture structure. This decision clarifies the treatment of operational debts in corporate insolvency cases.
articles.raw_summary: NCLAT held that a joint venture arrangement did not prevent insolvency proceedings where separate agreements clearly imposed supply obligations and payment responsibilities between parties. Tribunal ruled that unpaid supply dues constituted operational debt under IBC. The post NCLAT Upholds Section 9 Admission as Separate Supply Obligations Created Operational Debt appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Inheritance Isn’t a Birthright When a Valid Will Exists: Will Power Beats Bloodline: Supreme Court Upholds Sister’s Inheritance Despite Wife & Children’s Challenge
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld a will that favored the testator’s sister over his wife and children. The Court ruled that excluding natural heirs alone does not make a will suspicious if it is properly executed. This decision reinforces the legal principle that a valid will takes precedence over bloodline inheritance rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court upheld a Will executed in favour of the testator’s sister despite objections from his wife and children. The Court ruled that exclusion of natural heirs alone is not a suspicious circumstance when the Will is properly proved. articles.title: Income From AOP Held Non-Taxable in Member’s Hands as It Was Share of Profit: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that income received by members from an Association of Persons (AOP) as a share of profit is not taxable in their hands. The court upheld that such receipts constitute profit share rather than revenue share, preventing double taxation. This decision impacts how AOPs and their members are taxed under Indian law.
articles.raw_summary: SC examined nature of amounts received from an AOP and upheld findings that receipts constituted profit share rather than revenue share. Court noted that such income could not be taxed again in member’s hands. The post Income From AOP Held Non-Taxable in Member’s Hands as It Was Share of Profit: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

The rules aim to enhance transparency and fairness in insolvency cases.

articles.title: No More Technical Escapes: SC Settles Law on Guarantee Validity, Stamping & Disclosures in CIRP
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that liabilities from corporate guarantees qualify as financial debt under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code. This means lenders providing guarantees can now be recognized as financial creditors and included in the Committee of Creditors during insolvency resolution processes. The decision resolves ambiguities around guarantee validity, stamping requirements, and disclosure norms in Corporate Insolvency Resolution Proceedings (CIRP).
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that liabilities arising from corporate guarantees qualify as financial debt under Section 5(8) of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code. Consortium lenders were therefore entitled to Financial Creditor status and inclusion in the Committee of Creditors. The post No More Technical Escapes: SC Settles Law on Guarantee Validity, Stamping   Disclosures in CIRP appeared first on TaxGuru .

This judgment grants consortium lenders the status of Financial Creditors, strengthening their role in insolvency proceedings. The case involved China Development Bank and Doha Bank. The decision impacts how financial debts are classified and handled in insolvency cases.

articles.title: Retrospective GST Amendment Nullifies SC ITC Relief on Construction Costs
articles.summary: The Finance Act, 2025 amended Section 17(5)(d) of the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act retroactively, overturning a Supreme Court judgment that allowed Input Tax Credit (ITC) on certain commercial buildings. The amendment reverts to the earlier interpretation from July 2017, effectively nullifying the tax relief granted by the court. The change impacts businesses involved in construction and real estate, as it alters the tax treatment of input credits on construction costs.

articles.title: NCLAT says NCLT cannot directly order SFIO Probe; refers matter to Centre
articles.summary: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) ruled that the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) cannot directly order an investigation by the Serious Fraud Investigation Office (SFIO). This decision impacts how corporate fraud cases are handled and sets a legal precedent for tribunal and investigative agency roles.

articles.title: SC: Interest on Borrowed Funds Allowed Even for Investment Through Group Concerns – Commercial Expediency Prevails
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that interest paid on borrowed funds is deductible under Section 36(1)(iii) of the Income Tax Act if used for business purposes, even when invested through group concerns. The Court emphasized that commercial expediency justifies such deductions. This decision impacts how businesses claim tax benefits on loans for investments.

articles.title: Stamp Duty Value Cannot Be Adopted Without DVO Reference When Property Title Is Disputed
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that the Assessing Officer cannot adopt the stamp duty value of a property for tax purposes when the property title is disputed, unless a District Valuation Officer (DVO) examines the valuation. The tribunal restored the addition under Section 56(2)(x) to the Assessing Officer for reconsideration after objections about defective title, encroachments, and legal disputes. The ruling emphasizes the need for proper valuation examination in cases of disputed property ownership.
articles.raw_summary: Tribunal ruled that objections relating to defective title, encroachments, and legal disputes require proper valuation examination through a DVO reference. The addition under Section 56(2)(x) was therefore restored to the Assessing Officer for reconsideration. articles.title: Environment Ministry’s expert panel clears coal mining project expansion, despite 11 pending court cases
articles.summary: An expert panel under India's Environment Ministry has approved the expansion of a coal mining project, despite 11 pending court cases. The project involves diverting 628.09 hectares of forest land, with 276.04 hectares already cleared and the remaining 352.05 hectares awaiting final approval. The decision highlights tensions between industrial expansion and environmental regulations.
articles.raw_summary: The project involves 628.09 ha of forest land. Out of this, Stage-II forest clearance has been given to 276.04 ha, but the remaining 352.05 ha is yet to get the final nod for diversion.

articles.title: RBI proposes stricter Basel III disclosure norms for banks to boost transparency
articles.summary: The RBI’s draft Pillar 3 framework seeks to strengthen market discipline by mandating banks to publish detailed qualitative and quantitative disclosures on risks, capital position and liquidity in a standardised format. Lenders will also need to archive these disclosures on their websites for at least 10 years.

articles.title: GSTAT Upholds Anti-Profiteering Demand as ITC Benefit Was Not Passed to Homebuyers
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) upheld an anti-profiteering demand against ITC, ruling that the company failed to pass on additional Input Tax Credit benefits to eligible homebuyers as required under Section 171 of the CGST Act. The tribunal ordered ITC to refund the profiteered amounts along with GST, interest, and penalties. This decision reinforces compliance with tax regulations and protects consumer rights in real estate transactions.
articles.raw_summary: GSTAT held that failure to pass on additional Input Tax Credit benefits to eligible homebuyers violated Section 171 of the CGST Act. The Tribunal ordered refund of profiteered amounts with GST, interest and penalty. The post GSTAT Upholds Anti-Profiteering Demand as ITC Benefit Was Not Passed to Homebuyers appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Cabinet expansion: IUML and VCK get their ministers Shahjahan and Vanni Arasu in Vijay's government
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu government, led by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay, expanded its Cabinet by inducting two new ministers: AM Shahjahan from the Indian Union Muslim League (IUML) and Vanni Arasu from the Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi (VCK). This move strengthens the coalition government's representation across alliance partners. The expansion reflects ongoing efforts to broaden political support within the state's ruling alliance.
articles.raw_summary: IUML's AM Shahjahan and VCK's Vanni Arasu were sworn in as ministers in Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay's expanding Cabinet on Friday, as the TVK-led government continues to broaden its coalition with fresh inductions across alliance partners.

articles.title: Advocate Devvrat Elected As SCAORA President; Nikhil Jain Vice President
articles.summary: Advocate Devvrat has been elected as the President of the Supreme Court Advocates-on-Record Association (SCAORA), defeating rival advocate Shivaji M. Jadhav in a Thursday election. Nikhil Jain was elected Vice President, and Yugandhara Pawar Jha as Secretary. The counting for other positions is pending. This election is significant as SCAORA represents legal professionals who often engage with the Supreme Court and may influence legal or regulatory matters in India.
articles.raw_summary: Advocate Devvrat has been elected as the President of the Supreme Court Advocates-on-Record Association in the election held on Thursday (May 21) Advocate Nikhil Jain was elected as the Vice President. �Advocate Yugandhara Pawar Jha is the Secretary. Devvrat defeated advocate Shivaji M. Jadhav in the contest for the president's post. The counting for the posts of Treasurer, Joint Secretary, Joint Treasurer and Executive Members is yet to take place.

articles.title: Govt relaxes insolvency rules for MSMEs
articles.summary: The Indian government has relaxed insolvency rules for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) to simplify the process. Starting in 2026, only one valuer will be appointed per asset class to reduce costs, and strict new regulations will prevent conflicts of interest in pre-packaged resolutions. Related parties, recent auditors, and associates of resolution professionals are now barred from appointment.
articles.raw_summary: New rules aim to simplify insolvency for MSMEs. Only one valuer will be appointed per asset class, cutting costs. Strict new regulations prevent conflicts of interest in pre-packaged resolutions. Related parties, recent auditors, and associates of resolution professionals are now barred from appointment. These changes are effective from 2026.

The action follows the government's effort to enforce existing legal protections for employees.

articles.summary: Pranjul Bhandari, Chief India Economist at HSBC, believes India’s biggest challenge currently is pressure on its external balances. articles.title: GSTAT Orders Physical Service Due to Failure of Portal & Email Communication
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) directed that notices be served physically via registered or speed post after attempts to send them through the portal and email failed to elicit responses from the Department. The Tribunal relied on Section 169 of the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act, which allows cumulative modes of service to ensure proper adjudication and adherence to natural justice principles.
articles.raw_summary: GSTAT directed service through registered post/speed post after notices sent via portal and email failed to receive any effective response from the Department. The Tribunal held that Section 169 of the CGST Act permits cumulative modes of service to ensure proper adjudication and natural justice. articles.title: Customary Units Like Inches & Feet Can Be Used Alongside SI Units Under Legal Metrology Law
articles.summary: The Department of Consumer Affairs has clarified that customary units like inches, feet, and dozen can be used alongside SI metric units as supplementary declarations under the Legal Metrology Act. However, SI units will remain the legally recognized standard. This decision impacts businesses and consumers who may now use both measurement systems in trade and commerce.

articles.title: DVO Valuation Alone Cannot Justify Section 69 Addition Without Extra Payment Proof: ITAT Amritsar
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Amritsar ruled that a valuation report alone cannot justify tax additions under Section 69 of the Income Tax Act without proof of extra payments beyond the registered sale deed value. The tribunal deleted the tax addition after finding no evidence of unaccounted money. This decision sets a precedent for future tax assessments, emphasizing the need for concrete proof of undervaluation or unaccounted transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Amritsar held that a valuation report by itself cannot justify addition under Section 69 without evidence of extra payment. The Tribunal deleted the addition after finding no proof of on-money beyond the registered sale deed value. The post DVO Valuation Alone Cannot Justify Section 69 Addition Without Extra Payment Proof: ITAT Amritsar appeared first on TaxGuru .

The analysis focuses on party politics and institutional responses, with potential implications for India's own political and policy landscape.

articles.title: Article 20 Protects Against Retrospective Punishment, Double Jeopardy & Self-Incrimination
articles.summary: Article 20 of the Indian Constitution protects individuals from retrospective punishment, double jeopardy, and self-incrimination. Judicial precedents have reaffirmed that penal laws apply prospectively in criminal matters, ensuring fair legal treatment. This constitutional safeguard is a cornerstone of India's legal system, preventing authorities from imposing harsher punishments than those in place at the time of an offense.
articles.raw_summary: The constitutional safeguard under Article 20 prevents authorities from imposing harsher punishments than those prescribed when the offence occurred. Judicial precedents reaffirmed that penal laws operate prospectively in criminal matters. The post Article 20 Protects Against Retrospective Punishment, Double Jeopardy   Self-Incrimination appeared first on TaxGuru .

Experts suggest it may eventually become mandatory to curb malpractices and aid reconciliation.

Additionally, PFRDA is engaging with farmers, MSMEs, and self-help groups to promote retirement savings.

The directive follows the state's stance that bike taxis operate outside legal frameworks.

The decision reflects the state's commitment to enhancing regional infrastructure under central government oversight.

articles.title: West Bengal | No more religion-based doles; CM Adhikari forms anti-corruption and women's panels
articles.summary: The West Bengal Cabinet, led by Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, has approved the formation of two new commissions: an anti-corruption panel and a women's panel. The government will also stop providing financial aid based on religion and will directly transfer ₹3,000 to beneficiaries under the Annapurna women's welfare scheme starting June 1.

articles.title: Two judgments, no single standard: Why terror law UAPA’s bail threshold isn’t a straight line
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has delivered conflicting judgments on bail provisions under India's stringent anti-terror law, the UAPA. One ruling emphasizes strict conditions for bail, while another prioritizes individual liberty. This contradiction has reignited debates over which legal standard should govern UAPA cases, with implications for fundamental rights and judicial consistency in terror-related prosecutions.
articles.raw_summary: SC’s contradiction over bail in terror cases has revived questions over whether the stringent Watali test or the liberty-focused Najeeb ruling should guide UAPA jurisprudence.

articles.title: India, six others flags UK steel curbs at WTO
articles.summary: India and six nations have approached the World Trade Organization regarding the UK's new steel import restrictions. These measures, set to reduce tariff-free quotas significantly, are a concern for trade. India has urged the UK to adopt less trade-restrictive solutions. The issue is a hurdle for the India-UK economic pact.

articles.title: AI Promises Efficiency but the Fashion Industry Needs a Political Reckoning
articles.summary: Fashion for Good's report outlines a vision for an AI-driven fashion supply chain by 2036, emphasizing technical feasibility and operational seriousness. However, it raises unaddressed concerns about labour shifts, legal responsibility, and regulatory gaps that could either drive progress or perpetuate existing systemic failures. The discussion underscores the need for policy frameworks to manage AI's societal and economic impacts in industries like fashion.
articles.raw_summary: Ten years is a long horizon in any industry, but in AI it may be a category error. Fashion for Good's mapping of a 2036 AI-native supply chain is technically grounded and operationally serious. The structural questions it leaves unanswered, around rebound effects, labour transition, legal accountability, and regulatory reach, are the ones that will determine whether the vision it describes produces genuine progress or faster versions of the same systemic failures.

The company has not disclosed details such as the number of shares or pricing. This move allows the firm to prepare for the IPO privately. The development could influence discussions around cryptocurrency regulation in India, where policy on digital assets remains under review.
articles.raw_summary: A major cryptocurrency exchange has taken a significant step towards going public. The company has confidentially filed for an initial public offering in the United States. This move allows them to prepare for the IPO away from public scrutiny. Details on the number of shares and pricing are yet to be announced.

articles.title: The mammal wrapped in a shield of scales

articles.title: Storms and lightning kill over 100 in Uttar Pradesh
articles.summary: A severe storm with heavy rain and lightning struck several districts in Uttar Pradesh on May 13, killing 111 people and injuring 72 others. The incident highlights the need for disaster preparedness and response measures.

articles.title: Shrinking forest corridors in central India could disrupt tiger dispersal

articles.summary: A landslide on the Sonprayag-Gaurikund highway in Uttarakhand’s Rudraprayag district blocked the route to Kedarnath, trapping over 10,000 pilgrims. Heavy rain and rockfalls triggered the incident, prompting rescue operations by the State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) and National Disaster Response Force (NDRF). The event underscores risks to critical infrastructure in hilly regions, particularly during monsoon season.
articles.raw_summary: More than 10,000 Kedarnath pilgrims were safely evacuated after a landslide blocked the Sonprayag-Gaurikund highway in Uttarakhand’s Rudraprayag amid heavy rain and rockfalls Tuesday.

articles.title: Southwest monsoon reaches Andaman and Nicobar region ahead of Kerala onset: IMD
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported that the southwest monsoon has reached the Andaman and Nicobar Islands ahead of its expected onset in Kerala on May 26. The monsoon's advance over the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and the Andaman region is seen as favorable for further progress in the coming days. This development is significant for India's agricultural planning and water resource management, as the monsoon plays a crucial role in the country's farming cycles and overall economy.
articles.raw_summary: The southwest monsoon has advanced into parts of the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal and the Andaman region, the IMD said, adding that conditions remain favourable for further progress over the next few days ahead of the expected Kerala onset on May 26.

articles.raw_summary: Animal rights activist Maneka Gandhi said India can sharply reduce stray dog attacks and population levels within two years through proper implementation of district-level sterilisation centres, while advocate Nanita Sharma questioned authorities’ commitment to enforcing long-pending animal birth control rules after the Supreme Court’s latest directions.

articles.title: Former Uttarakhand CM B C Khanduri dies at 91 after prolonged illness
articles.summary: Former Uttarakhand chief minister and senior BJP leader B C Khanduri passed away at 91 following prolonged illness. A retired Army major general, Khanduri was known for his disciplined leadership, clean image, and contribution to India’s highway infrastructure through the Golden Quadrilateral project.
articles.raw_summary: Former Uttarakhand chief minister and senior BJP leader B C Khanduri passed away at 91 following prolonged illness. A retired Army major general, Khanduri was known for his disciplined leadership, clean image, and contribution to India’s highway infrastructure through the Golden Quadrilateral project.

articles.title: 800 kg contaminated khoya destroyed in Meerut: Here's how to spot adulteration
articles.summary: Food safety officials in Meerut seized nearly 70 tins of refined oil and 8 sacks of skimmed milk powder during a raid. The operation targeted contaminated khoya (a dairy product) weighing 800 kg, which was destroyed. Food adulteration poses health risks and may prompt regulatory bodies like FSSAI to strengthen enforcement or introduce stricter measures for dairy and edible oil sectors.
articles.raw_summary: The food safety team confiscated nearly 70 tins of refined oil and 8 sacks of skimmed milk powder during the operation.

articles.title: Indian Railways okays ₹2,193 crore projects for Chennai suburban network, Howrah-Delhi route and Katra section
articles.summary: The projects include the ₹993 crore Arakkonam-Chengalpattu doubling project, the ₹962 crore Kiul-Jhajha third line project and ₹238 crore worth of tunnel rehabilitation and bridge protection works.

articles.title: Messi tour organiser files plaint against ex-Bengal minister, former top cop for 'sabotaging' event
articles.summary: The organiser of Lionel Messi's upcoming tour in India, Satadru Dutta, has filed a police complaint accusing former West Bengal minister Aroop Biswas and former police chief Rajeev Kumar of causing chaos at the 2025 Salt Lake stadium event. Dutta alleges they sabotaged the event and made him a scapegoat. The incident raises questions about administrative and legal accountability in managing large-scale public events.
articles.raw_summary: Lionel Messi tour organiser Satadru Dutta files police complaint accusing Aroop Biswas, Rajeev Kumar and others of causing 2025 Salt Lake stadium chaos and making him a scapegoat

articles.title: ‘Ugly-looking statue’: West Bengal sports minister announces removal of Salt Lake Stadium sculpture
articles.summary: West Bengal's sports minister announced the removal of a controversial sculpture at the Salt Lake Stadium, calling it 'ugly-looking.' The structure, installed in 2017 ahead of the FIFA U-17 World Cup, features footballing legs with a football on its torso and the Biswa Bangla logo. The decision reflects the state government's authority over public infrastructure and aesthetics in state-owned facilities.
articles.raw_summary: The structure, featuring footballing legs with a football on torso and Biswa Bangla logo, was set up in 2017 ahead of the 2017 FIFA U-17 World Cup.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked again; Kolkata sees highest rise in fuel rates

articles.title: SC grants bail to man accused of posing as PM Modi aide to extort money
articles.summary: The Supreme Court granted bail to Mohammad Kashif in a money laundering case investigated by the Enforcement Directorate, noting that he had already spent nearly three years in custody. The case involves allegations that Kashif used morphed photographs with Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union ministers to falsely project political influence and collect money from people.

articles.title: Noida 'Dowry' Death: What were Twisha Sharma’s final messages to her mother
articles.summary: Since several days before her death, Twisha Sharma, a former Miss Pune, had been sending distressing Whatsapp messages to her mother, which NDTV has accessed. Now, her family has accused her husband Samarth Singh and his mother Giribala Singh, a judge, of mental harassment and murder, and demanded a second post-mortem.

articles.title: Rajnath Singh heads to Vietnam, South Korea to boost defence, Indo-Pacific ties
articles.summary: India's Defence Minister Rajnath Singh will visit Vietnam from May 18 to 19 and South Korea from May 19 to 21 to enhance defence industrial partnerships and strengthen Indo-Pacific ties. The visit aims to boost bilateral cooperation in defence and security, reflecting India's strategic focus on the region. This follows recent diplomatic engagements and underscores India's proactive foreign policy in the Indo-Pacific.
articles.raw_summary: The minister will be in Vietnam from May 18 to 19 and in South Korea from May 19 to 21 to "strengthen defence industrial partnerships," among other things.

articles.title: Supreme Court says ‘bail is rule’ even in UAPA cases, doubts own orders in Umar Khalid, Sharjeel Imam cases

articles.title: Bengal: Major anti-encroachment drive conducted to clear out Howrah station area
articles.summary: The West Bengal government conducted a large-scale anti-encroachment operation outside Howrah railway station, demolishing illegal shops and structures along the Ganga ghat to the station premises. Officials stated the drive was carried out past midnight to clear unauthorized constructions. The action highlights enforcement of urban planning and railway infrastructure regulations.
articles.raw_summary: A massive anti-encroachment drive was carried out outside Howrah railway station past midnight, with bulldozers demolishing rows of makeshift shops and illegal structures along the stretch from the Ganga ghat to the station premises, officials said on Sunday.

articles.title: K T Rama Rao demands Bandi Sanjay’s removal for fair probe after son’s arrest in POCSO case
articles.summary: BRS Working President K T Rama Rao has demanded the immediate removal of Union minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar after his son was arrested in a Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) case. Rao alleged that political influence could interfere with the investigation. The demand underscores concerns about the fairness of legal proceedings involving public officials' family members.
